h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner seeks to understand the physical testing criteria after moving past the checklist.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Use this mark. Small flex that returns when the load comes off is movement, not slip.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts by introducing a physical tape mark to operationalize the abstract distinction between movement and slip.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists to trigger recognition-contingent uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists to trigger recognition-contingent uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Use this mark. Small flex that returns when the load comes off is movement, not slip.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts by introducing a physical tape mark to operationalize the abstract distinction between movement and slip.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Use this mark. Small flex that returns when the load comes off is movement, not slip.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts by introducing a physical tape mark to operationalize the abstract distinction between movement and slip.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects the geometric construction to successfully demonstrate a doubled area by simply doubling the side length.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick. Say the two claims separately.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's frustration by explicitly validating the failure of the evidence and restructuring the task into two separate claims.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly takes up the learner's realization that their evidence failed and uses it to reframe the geometric proof.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly takes up the learner's realization that their evidence failed and uses it to reframe the geometric proof.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick. Say the two claims separately.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's frustration by explicitly validating the failure of the evidence and restructuring the task into two separate claims.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Then the evidence has turned against that claim. It is not a drawing trick. Say the two claims separately.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's frustration by explicitly validating the failure of the evidence and restructuring the task into two separate claims.
